titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Robert K. Greenleaf Papers
titlestmt.author: Andover Newton Theological School and Yale Divinity School Library staff

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday June 3, 2026 at 18:00

did.abstract: The collection documents Greenleaf's professional life, including as founder of the Servant Leadership movement and of the Greenleaf Center for Servant Leadership, which was first known as the Center for Applied Ethics.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Greenleaf was born in 1904 in Terre Haute, Indiana. He attended Rose Polytechnic (an engineering school) in Terre Haute for several years, then transferred to Carleton College in Minnesota. He graduated as a math major in 1926 and took a job with AT and T (telephone company). During his 38-year career with the company he served as Director of Management Development, originator of the world's first corporate assessment center, promotion of the women and Black workers to non-menial positions, a program to expose up-and-coming leaders to the humanities, even bringing in famous theologians and psychologists to speak about the wider implications of corporate decisions.

Greenleaf retired in 1964 and began his second career as a writer, consultant, and teacher. His seminal essay "The Servant as Leader" was published in 1970. In 1976, Paulist Press published a book of his essays titled "Servant Leadership." He died in 1990.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from Andover Newton Theological School Collection, 2017; Gift of Jeffrey D. Jones, 2023

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection includes autobiographical material, correspondence, subject files, writings, and memorabilia. Correspondence from Jeffrey D. Jones dating from the 1980s was added to the collection in 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I. Journal and Autobiographical Material

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Robert K. Greenleaf Papers (RG 287), Special Collections, Yale Divinity Library.

did.unittitle: Notebook and loose pages of journal and dream notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes from work with Jungian analysts Ira Progoff and Martha Jaeger.

did.unittitle: Two notebooks of dream notes - DB#1, DB#2
Audiocassette tape related to sessions of Greenleaf with Ira Progoff
Notes related to "hole in the hedge philosophy"
Handwritten narrative of life and work through 1920s - 2 drafts
Handwritten narrative: "My work with AT&T" - 2 drafts
Typescript narratives: "My work in education", "My work with churches"
Handwritten and typescript narratives: "My work with foundations"
Handwritten and typescript narratives: "My work in India"
Typescript narrative: "Age 40-60: What I did with those years"
Handwritten narrative of life and work after age 60
Handwritten narrative: "The institution as servant, reflections on experience"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See more correspondence in the Subject Files.

did.unittitle: Correspondence concerning Greenleaf and the Greenleaf Center

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Winter 1990-91 issue of The Servant Leader, newsletter of the Robert K. Greenleaf Center, with tribute to Greenleaf.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a more detailed listing of contents of subject files see the original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

did.unittitle: The Servant as A Person (Jefferson House)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a full list of titles see original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

did.unittitle: Untitled, "Abortion - "Notes on community in the Late Twentieth Century"
"Notes on Institute of Chairing" - "You Need To Be Human"
An Opportunity for a Foundation Concern in the Field of Leadership
What are you called to do? A sense of Vocation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a full list of publications by Greenleaf see the original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

did.unittitle: International certificates of vaccination and personal health history: Robert K. and Esther H. Greenleaf
Passports: Robert K. and Esther H. Greenleaf

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letter of Greenleaf to his mother, report cards

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Carleton College handbook and directories, letters regarding summer employment and the Men's Glee Club

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents re marriage license and certificate, poem to commemorate retirement from Dartmouth program

did.unittitle: Material re honorary degree awarded by Carleton College
Obituaries and memorials of Robert K. Greenleaf

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including by Ed Ouelette, Alfonso Narvaez, and memorial service program at Bangor Theological Seminary

did.unittitle: Microfilm prepared for preservation purposes by Andover Newton Theological School Library (9 positive and 9 negative reels plus one extra positive copy of reel 7)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Labels on the microfilm correlate with the original finding aid for the Greenleaf Papers, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.
